The contractors who win in the Greater Boston map pack are the ones who answer the phone at 9 PM, capture seasonal valleys with paid, and convert reputation into ranking velocity.

sections.heading: Why most contractor marketing leaks money.
sections.intro: The same four problems show up across HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and roofing engagements. They're not technical — they're operational.

cards.title: Missed calls at peak demand
cards.body: Emergency calls (no heat at 11 PM, water leak Sunday morning) hit voicemail. The customer dials the next contractor on the SERP and books with whoever answers.

cards.title: Seasonal spend mismatch
cards.body: Heating ads still running mid-July. Landscaping ads off in February when early-bird booking is highest. Generic agencies don't tune for MA seasonality.

cards.title: Reputation invisible online
cards.body: 30 years of word-of-mouth. 14 Google reviews from 2019. The contractor who's actually best in town isn't ranking — and the customer can't tell.

cards.title: Service area too narrow or too broad
cards.body: GBP service area set to one town when crew serves 8. Or set to 30 towns when the competitive density makes ranking impossible. Both kill efficiency.

sections.heading: The starting playbook for most MA contractors.
sections.intro: Local SEO + GBP first because they compound. AI receptionist second because missed calls are an ROI emergency. Reviews third. Ads as a layer for seasonality.

cards.title: Local SEO
cards.body: Service-area optimization across the 5–8 towns you actually serve. GBP + citations + on-site local content. Real ranking gains typically take 3–6 months — it's a build, not an overnight switch.

cards.title: AI Receptionist
cards.body: Answered fast, 24/7 — the AI greets callers right away. Books on your dispatch tool live. Catches the after-hours calls that would otherwise go to voicemail.

cards.title: Reputation
cards.body: Post-job SMS + email automation. Surface the word-of-mouth that's already there with a steady flow of new Google reviews.

sections.heading: What a contractor engagement looks like.

steps.title: Service-area + GBP audit
steps.body: Map real service area. GBP audit, citation audit, review audit, top-5 competitor benchmark. Identify 3 quick wins.

steps.title: Foundation
steps.body: GBP rebuild, citation cleanup, on-site service-area pages (genuinely unique per town), AI receptionist install.

steps.title: Reputation + content
steps.body: Review automation live. A steady flow of new reviews starts coming in. First service-page content rounds shipped.

steps.title: Strategy review
steps.body: Add Google Ads in months 2–3 to fill seasonal valleys. Tune service-area + ad budget against demand cycle.

sections.heading: Why home-services SEO compounds harder than most verticals.
sections.prose_html:
Most service businesses in Massachusetts have one disadvantage and one advantage when it comes to digital marketing. The disadvantage: customer LTV is moderate ($300–$2,000 per job typically), so each acquisition has to be cheap. The advantage: demand is real, recurring, and not negotiable — when the heat goes out at 6 PM in February, somebody is going to fix it tonight.

The contractor who shows up reliably in the local map pack, answers the phone within 2 rings (or has an AI that does), and has a recent flow of 4-star+ reviews captures a disproportionate share of that demand. The work is operational, not creative. The marketing playbook is more like running a clean kitchen than running a brand campaign.

Service-area pages — the right way

The previous generation of agencies built 200 templated city pages and got their clients penalized in Google's Helpful Content Update. We don't do that. We build 5–8 genuinely unique service-area pages per contractor — one per town actually served, with real photos of jobs done there, real testimonials from that town, and real local content. Slower to produce, but sustainable.

The seasonality math

Massachusetts contractor demand has predictable cycles. HVAC heating peaks November–February; cooling peaks June–August. Landscaping inverse. Plumbing has emergency baseline + remodel-driven peaks April–June and September–October. Electrical follows construction cycles. We forecast from your historical job data and pre-build 30–60 days early — both for SEO content and for ad campaign launches.

aside_items.note_body: HVAC (heating + cooling + IAQ), plumbing (residential + light commercial), electrical (residential + service work), roofing + siding, landscaping + outdoor, general contractors + remodelers, painters, pest control, junk removal, cleaning services. Tuned playbook per vertical.

Massachusetts contractor licensing is consumer-facing — license number, HIC registration, and insurance proof are direct trust signals. We surface these prominently in GBP, schema, and on-site to lift conversion alongside ranking.

Most MA contractors start with our Smart Website & Marketing Package at $500/mo (a 12-month agreement) — website, Local SEO, Google Business Profile, reviews, and the AI front desk in one plan. When you're ready to grow faster, add accelerators: SEO at $1,299/mo for content and link building, Google Ads management at $1,299/mo, or Meta Ads at $999/mo (ad spend separate). We confirm the right mix at your free audit.

items.q: Do you work with HVAC / plumbing / electrical contractors?
items.a: Yes — these are the densest part of our client base. Each vertical has a tuned playbook (heating-cycle SEO for HVAC, emergency-search optimization for plumbing, construction-cycle alignment for electrical).

items.q: Can you handle multi-town service areas?
items.a: Yes — most MA contractors serve 5–8 adjacent towns from one office. We build the GBP service area + per-town landing pages correctly so you rank in adjacent towns from one address. Without templating that gets you penalized.

items.q: How do you handle seasonal demand cycles?
items.a: Forecast from your historical job data. Pre-build SEO content 30–60 days early. Launch Google Ads campaigns ahead of the demand peak. Most contractors smooth their revenue curve significantly within the first 12 months.

items.q: Will I rank in Boston / Cambridge as a Woburn contractor?
items.a: Yes if you actually serve those markets. GBP service area + on-site city pages + properly attributed citations let one contractor address rank in 4–7 adjacent towns, including Boston neighborhoods. Most clients see meaningful movement in 3–6 months.

items.q: What's a typical contractor engagement size?
items.a: Most contractors start with our Smart Website & Marketing Package at $500/mo (a 12-month agreement), which bundles Local SEO, Google Business Profile, reviews, and the AI receptionist. When you're ready to grow faster, add accelerators: SEO at $1,299/mo for content and link building, Google Ads management at $1,299/mo, or Meta Ads at $999/mo (ad spend separate). We confirm the right mix at your free audit.

items.q: Do you do dispatch / CRM integration?
items.a: Yes — Jobber, Housecall Pro, ServiceTitan, FieldEdge, and similar. Lead routing, calendar booking, and review-request triggers all wire into your existing dispatch stack. No double entry.
